title: Three-hand stitched clock demo
description: Build and validate demo_three_hand_clock.py using bounded stitched upload + phase-locked segments. Includes manual visual acceptance.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: In progress: implementing demo_three_hand_clock.py with phase-locked segments, bounded frame budget, and upload fallback handling.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented demo_three_hand_clock.py + tests/docs. Smoke run on real device succeeded with --once --fps 4 --segment-seconds 10 (uploaded 40 frames; no retries). Next: user visual confirmation for continuous second-hand motion and time correctness.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updated three-hand clock demo to default no-loading push delivery; stitched remains optional. Awaiting user visual confirmation that loading flashes are gone.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying visual polish tweak: default hand colors updated to hour=60% gray, minute=50% gray, second=40% gray for demo_three_hand_clock.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Completed hand color default change in demo_three_hand_clock: hour=(153,153,153), minute=(128,128,128), second=(102,102,102). Restarted live demo for visual confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added Radix color token support for clock styling workflow so hand/dial colors can use tokens like gray11, dark.gray11, and grayDark11.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Completed: three-hand clock demo implemented, no-loading push delivery defaulted, hand color tuning applied, and Radix color token support integrated. Closing task.
